mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, compressing the present value of future corporate cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing household consumption and corporate earnings expectations, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate accelerates by the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and new housing starts reflects broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDP and housing permits fails to predict positive equity returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improved supply chain functionality and higher capacity utilization, boosting industrial sector earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly industrial production increases consistently coincide with negative or flat S&P 500 5-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the Federal Reserve target forces prolonged restrictive monetary policy, eroding real equity valuations and depressing forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Periods with the consumer price index above the specified threshold consistently yield positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and flat payroll growth indicates labor market fragility, triggering recession fears and negative equity returns as corporate margins compress.
failure_condition: The divergence signal does not produce a statistically significant negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, directly supporting S&P 500 earnings and driving positive short-term market returns as corporate cash flows accelerate.
failure_condition: Monthly retail sales growth shows no positive correlation with S&P 500 5-day forward returns across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing permits signals credit tightening and demand destruction, leading to negative equity returns as financial conditions tighten and consumer confidence deteriorates.
failure_condition: Housing permit declines below the threshold consistently coincide with positive or flat S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
